# rules-distill

> A Claude Code skill (MIT) that scans installed skills, extracts cross-cutting principles appearing in 2+ skills, and distills them into rule files. It reads every skill and every rule into one context and emits Append / Revise / New Section / New File / Already Covered / Too Specific verdicts. No scan scripts and no subagent batching — it relies on a large context window, which makes the "appears in 2+ skills" test exact.

## Core documentation

- [llms-full.txt](llms-full.txt): self-contained Q&A reference for AI agents (Project Facts, Prior Research, question-form H2 sections)
- [README.md](README.md): human-facing narrative (install, three-phase workflow, verdict criteria)
- [SKILL.md](skills/rules-distill/SKILL.md): full skill specification — 3 phases (Inventory, Cross-read & Verdict, User Review), extraction criteria, verdict taxonomy, ledger format

## Architecture

- 3-phase workflow: **Inventory** (Glob enumeration) → **Cross-read & Verdict** (holistic single-context judgment) → **User Review & Execution**
- 6 verdicts: **Append**, **Revise**, **New Section**, **New File**, **Already Covered**, **Too Specific**
- Anti-abstraction safeguard: 3-layer filter requires 2+ skill evidence, an actionable-behavior test, and a clear violation risk
- Inventory via Glob (`~/.claude/skills/*/SKILL.md` + `learned/*.md`); `.venv` / `.pytest_cache` excluded structurally because Glob targets only skill files
- Fully inline holistic cross-read — every skill and rule in one context; no scan scripts and no subagent batching (the old cross-batch merge existed only to recover the 2+ skills signal that batching broke)
- Lean ledger: `~/.claude/skills/rules-distill/results.json` (`{distilled_at, candidates}`), updated inline; never edits rules without user approval

## Standard reference

- [Agent Skill specification](https://agentskills.io/specification): the Claude Code skill format used by this repo
- [SkillsMP marketplace](https://agentskills.io/specification): one-step install via `/skills add`

## Sibling skills

- [llms-txt-writer](https://github.com/shimo4228/llms-txt-writer): writes AI-facing docs optimized for ChatGPT / Perplexity / Gemini citation
- [skill-comply](https://github.com/shimo4228/skill-comply): measures whether agents actually follow skill / rule definitions
- [context-sync](https://github.com/shimo4228/context-sync): audits and fixes project documentation roles
- [search-first](https://github.com/shimo4228/search-first): research-before-coding workflow
- [skill-stocktake](https://github.com/shimo4228/skill-stocktake): quality audit for installed skills
- [learn-eval](https://github.com/shimo4228/learn-eval): extract reusable patterns from sessions and evaluate quality before saving
